headline: Airline stocks hinge on pricing power as carriers push through fare hikes, UBS says
summary: Shares of major US airlines including United Airlines Holdings Inc (NASDAQ:UAL, XETRA:UAL1), Delta Air Lines Inc (NYSE:DAL), American Airlines Group Inc (NASDAQ:AAL, XETRA:A1G) and Alaska Air Group (NYSE:ALK) are increasingly tied to their ability to sustain aggressive fare increases as fuel...

headline: Market Madness: Why JPMorgan, McDonald's are slam dunk stock picks
summary: Tipping off Yahoo Finance's "Market Madness" bracket tournament, Winthrop Capital CIO Adam Coons joins Julie Hyman to make the case for the first round of corporate match-ups, which includes JPMorgan Chase (JPM) vs. TJX (TJX) and McDonald's (MCD) vs. Disney (DIS).

headline: Delta Air Lines Announces Webcast of March Quarter 2026 Financial Results
summary: Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL) will hold a live conference call and webcast to discuss its March quarter 2026 financial results at 10 a.m. ET, Wednesday, April 8, 2026.

headline: Jim Cramer Highlights the Resilience of Delta Air Lines Despite Surging Oil Prices
summary: Delta Air Lines, Inc. (NYSE:DAL) is one of Jim Cramer’s latest stock calls as oil drops and the U.S. market rises. headline: Delta Air Lines price target raised to $77 from $71 at TD Cowen
summary: TD Cowen analyst Tom Fitzgerald raised the firm’s price target on Delta Air Lines (DAL) to $77 from $71 and keeps a Buy rating on the shares. The firm left yesterday’s conference presentations “encouraged” by the better than expected impact of fuel cost during Q1 and airline management commentary on forward bookings. TD upped estimates for the group to reflect improved pricing. United Airlines remains its top pick, followed by Delta and then Alaska Air. Claim 70% Off TipRanks PremiumUnlock hedge
